title: What am i thinking when i study
A guide to co-living with fantastic animals
description: This is my graduation project, an educational picture book aimed at children aged 6 to 8, introducing various mythological creatures by illustrating scenarios and potential issues that might arise when living with them.

title: Series
description: This series is an ongoing exploration where each illustration serves as an independent visual experiment.

title: Birdwatching Zine
description: A compact, pocket-sized manual designed for on￾the-go utility. It features intuitive illustrations and a clear typographic hierarchy, translating complex birdwatching techniques into an accessible and engaging visual experience.

title: The Words I Cannot Tell You
description: An evocative illustration inspired by Paul B. Preciado's writings on the transgender experience within the family dynamic during pandemic confinement. The work serves as a visual exegesis of Preciado's text, articulating the palpable tension between identity, language, and familial affection.

title: Food and body
description: Concept-driven illustrations centered on the multifaceted theme of Food and Body. My focus was on capturing the palpable visceral tension of identity construction through appetite, incorporating both intrusive and harmonious composition to reflect on self-perception. Utilizing an affective palette of flesh tones and contrasting flavors, I aimed to provoke a dialogue on how food is not merely consumption but a powerful, often chaotic, form of embodied expression.

title: The pain of body
description: A series of evocative illustrations created for the independent Italian magazine Lök Zine, exploring the multifaceted theme of 'Body'.

title: Leggerezza/Lightness
description: This conceptual illustration translates a fleeting moment of urban freedom. By contrasting the welcoming warmth of the station's interior with the enveloping dusk, I focused on capturing the kinetic energy of a girl dancing alone. The visual language utilizes fluid lines and soft rendering to communicate transient joy and grace. This project showcases my proficiency in utilizing dynamic composition and emotive coloring to tell a story within a single, poetic frame

title: L'uovo
description: This is my undergraduate graduation project, a picture book about a young girl who suddenly decides to hatch an egg herself, exploring themes of family upheaval and trauma.

title: Specchi di naufragi - Silvina Ocampo
description: A sequence of editorial illustrations developed created for the short story collection The Mirror of the Shipwreck by the Italian writer Silvina Ocampo.

title: Posters of the Birdwatching Festival
description:
This project defines the visual identity for a birdwatching festival, exploring the synergy between vector illustration and typographic layout to create an accessible, dynamic visual language.
The typography features a juxtaposition of a slender, elegant typeface with an organic, handwritten script. To enhance the tactile quality, I integrated handcrafted textures that evoke a sense of lightness and approachability. The color palette balances cool, luminous tones with earthy browns, directly referencing the natural environment.
The key vision centers on a horizontal poster inspired by community event banners. By adopting a more fluid and informal composition, I successfully avoided a rigid institutional tone while maintaining a clear information hierarchy to ensure readability.

title: The Chinese Tin Toys
description:
For the cover design, I drew inspiration from the visual architecture of vintage toy catalogs, emulating their signature layout and graphic style.
The color strategy directly references the product design of that era, characterized by high-saturation palettes and bold tonal contrasts. By prioritizing vibrant, warm hues, I created a dynamic and playful visual impact designed to capture immediate attention. This graphic approach successfully evokes a playful universe, establishing a seamless connection between the book's content and its visual storytelling.
 The color palette primarily utilizes vibrant red and luminous yellow in their purest tones. This deliberate combination draws upon the visual iconography associated with China, creating an immediate and intuitive link to the book's central theme.
 For the typography, I selected a clean, contemporary sans-serif typeface. To infuse the design with a playful character, I replaced specific elements within the letterforms with small stars, a graphic detail that not only reinforces the toy-inspired aesthetic but also adds a layer of whimsical charm to the overall composition.

title: HeySong Sarsaparilla
description:
This project features a comprehensive packaging redesign for a beloved Taiwanese soft drink. Known for its light, crisp, and effervescent profile, this canned soda appeals to a broad demographic, with a strong following among younger consumers. 
 To visually articulate the drink's refreshing qualities, the design incorporates geometric circular motifs and bubble-inspired textures. These elements act as a visual metaphor for the beverage's sparkle and fizz. Every typographic and color choice was strategically curated to project a modern, trend-driven image that resonates with the dynamic energy of today's youth culture.

title: Pure Rice Brewing
description:
This project involves the packaging design for a premium Junmai (pure rice) sake, encompassing both the bottle label and a custom carrier bag.
 My creative process began with a visual analysis of rice-based spirits, identifying a distinct trend toward delicate, desaturated color palettes: such as beige, white, and silver, to communicate purity and elegance.
 Adopting this direction, I utilized expansive minimalist surfaces to evoke a sense of cultural refinement. This aesthetic is complemented by a slender serif typeface and graphic motifs that highlight the graceful, elongated forms of rice grains, reinforcing the product's artisanal value and sophisticated character.
